reconstruction_test: If 2.34 were removed, could the engineering analog be reconstructed from 2.33 (if-then loss-trio) plus 2.35 (peer-witness sharpening)?
result: No — 2.33 names the kīrti-loss as part of an if-then trio without the avyaya frame; 2.35 sharpens to mahā-rathas as a specific witness-class. Neither carries the avyayām-mirror to the imperishable-Self language of 2.17 / 2.21. The 'imperishable disrepute' framing — that the same word for the Self's imperishability is used for the actor's manufactured signal — is irreducibly 2.34's contribution. The Kafka-to-Pulsar migration scenario specifically requires the avyayām framing to land — without 2.34's avyayām, the scenario would slide into either the if-then form (2.33) or the peer-witness form (2.35), but not the imperishable-signal form.
verdict_reason: The engineering layer carries 2.34-specific doctrinal load (avyayām-Self-mirror, sambhāvitasya restriction) that cannot be reconstructed from neighbours. PASS.
